[research_query from bm_anchor]
{'question': 'Evaluate MoS₂, WSe₂, and MoSe₂ as candidate monolayer components for 2D van der Waals heterostructure photovoltaic devices. Identify which material pairs have a band gap offset greater than 0.3 eV — a necessary condition for type-II band alignment that drives charge separation.', 'background': 'Transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) like MoS2, WSe2, and MoSe2 are atomically thin 2D semiconductors that form van der Waals heterostructures with interlayer charge transfer. Type-II (staggered) band alignment requires that the conduction and valence band offsets between layers drive electrons and holes to different layers, enabling charge separation. A band gap offset |delta_Eg| > 0.3 eV between layers is a necessary (not sufficient) condition for type-II alignment.', 'workflow_hint': 'Fetch band gap data for all three TMDs in parallel via formula lookup. Search PubMed for type-II heterostructure context. Use python-exec to compute pairwise |delta_Eg| for all three pairs and classify as TYPE2_CANDIDATE if > 0.3 eV.', 'query_summary': 'Evaluate MoS₂, WSe₂, and MoSe₂ as candidate monolayer components for 2D van der Waals heterostructure photovoltaic devices. Identify which material pairs have a band gap offset greater than 0.3 eV — a'}
